input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the `IsTestProject` property, which MSBuild sets to `true` automatically when a project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <!-- shared properties for all projects -->

  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why it works:** The `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package (which your test projects already reference) sets `IsTestProject=true`. Library/production projects never get that property, so the conditional block is skipped for them.

**Tip:** If the timing doesn't work in `Directory.Build.props` (the property is set later by NuGet imports), move the block to `Directory.Build.targets` instead — it's evaluated after package props are imported.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) runner properties only apply to test projects, not library/production projects.

This is a common pattern when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. The key is to use an MSBuild condition that detects whether a project is a test project.

The standard way to do this is to check for the `IsTestProject` property, which is set to `true` by the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package (or you can set it yourself). Another approach is to check if the project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`.


The cleanest approach is to wrap those properties with a condition that checks if `IsTestProject` is true — the test SDK sets this automatically, so it's the most reliable way to distinguish test projects from everything else.
